title: englerina gabonensis
intro: Englerina gabonensis, a species of plant in the family Apocynaceae, is a climbing shrub native to the tropical regions of Central and West Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of Englerina gabonensis include its potential to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections, as well as its use as a tonic to improve overall well-being. Herbal preparations of Englerina gabonensis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are typically prepared by steeping the plant's leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent, and are used to treat various health conditions.
